description:
You are acting as the database administrator of a legacy project.  
    All SQL utility scripts live in the directory  

        /home/user/query_scripts  

    and end with the extension “.sql”.  
    Three of these scripts still contain the non-performant pattern  

        SELECT *  

    which you must refactor to the explicit column list  

        SELECT id, name  

    1. Perform the replacement in-place on every “.sql” file under  
       /home/user/query_scripts **using one single** find + xargs command  
       (i.e. exactly one shell command line that combines “find” and “xargs”).  
       • The command must do the actual editing; additional commands are fine  
         for verification or logging, but the edit itself has to happen from  
         one find | xargs pipeline.  

    2. Immediately after the replacement, create a log file called  

        /home/user/query_scripts/refactor.log  

       The log must be plain text with **exactly** the following format so the
       automated grader can check it:

            <absolute-path-of-modified-file>
            <absolute-path-of-modified-file>
            … (one absolute path per line)

       • List every “.sql” file that was actually modified.  
       • Sort the paths in **lexicographic (ASCII) ascending order**.  
       • Do not include blank lines, trailing spaces, or any other text.  

    Example (illustrative only – actual paths shown below):

        /home/user/query_scripts/a.sql
        /home/user/query_scripts/b.sql
        /home/user/query_scripts/z.sql

    Hints you may find useful (do NOT echo these literally):
       • “find … -print0” together with “xargs -0” avoids problems with spaces.
       • “sed -i” can perform in-place editing.
       • You can pipe “printf '%s\n' …” or reuse the result of “grep -l”  
         if you want to build the log after the replacement.  

    The grader will verify the following:

       a) The strings “SELECT *” no longer occur in any “.sql” file under  
          /home/user/query_scripts.  
       b) In the three specific files that used to contain the pattern,  
          only the exact replacement “SELECT id, name” now exists instead.  
       c) /home/user/query_scripts/refactor.log exists, is world-readable, and
          contains exactly the absolute paths of the three modified files in
          the required sorted order, and nothing else.  

    Remember: the **editing itself** must be done via a single command line
    that combines find and xargs.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:

    1) /home/user/query_scripts/get_users.sql
       Initial contents:
           SELECT * FROM users WHERE active = 1;
           -- some comment

    2) /home/user/query_scripts/get_orders.sql
       Initial contents:
           SELECT id, total FROM orders;

    3) /home/user/query_scripts/update_users.sql
       Initial contents:
           UPDATE users SET active = 0 WHERE last_login < '2023-01-01';

    4) /home/user/query_scripts/archive_customers.sql
       Initial contents:
           SELECT * FROM customers WHERE archived = 0;

    5) /home/user/query_scripts/delete_tmp.sql
       Initial contents:
           SELECT * FROM tmp_data;

    Directory permissions: 0755.  
    File permissions: 0644.

    Expected state after successful task execution:

    a) /home/user/query_scripts/get_users.sql
       Contents become:
           SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE active = 1;
           -- some comment

    b) /home/user/query_scripts/get_orders.sql
       Remains unchanged.

    c) /home/user/query_scripts/update_users.sql
       Remains unchanged.

    d) /home/user/query_scripts/archive_customers.sql
       Contents become:
           SELECT id, name FROM customers WHERE archived = 0;

    e) /home/user/query_scripts/delete_tmp.sql
       Contents become:
           SELECT id, name FROM tmp_data;

    f) /home/user/query_scripts/refactor.log
       Must exist, mode 0644, containing exactly (including newline at end of each line):

           /home/user/query_scripts/archive_customers.sql
           /home/user/query_scripts/delete_tmp.sql
           /home/user/query_scripts/get_users.sql

       (Three lines, newline-terminated, no extra whitespace.)

    The grader will also search all *.sql files under /home/user/query_scripts
    and assert that the literal string “SELECT *” does not occur anywhere.
